description: Provider-side catalog contract for sellable industry solution packs and industry knowledge products.
catalog_principles: Industry solutions are deployable product packages, not loose prompts.
Industry knowledge assets are priced and licensed separately from the base Digital Office runtime.
Sold industry knowledge assets can be used only inside the provider-managed Digital Office product.
Sold industry knowledge assets must not be downloaded, exported, bulk-copied, or used by customer tools outside Digital Office.
Tenant documents and raw project knowledge are never mixed into provider-owned catalog assets.
Customer-contributed methodology enters the catalog only after tenant admin approval, anonymization, provider review, and licensing clearance.

industry_solution_pack.purpose: Deploy workflows, agent templates, rules, GUI screens, and evaluation tests for a target industry or role.

industry_knowledge_pack.purpose: Sell curated domain knowledge that can be mounted into company or project knowledge retrieval.

connector_pack.purpose: Enable customer-owned external knowledge sources through provider-validated connectors and skills.

mount_modes.company_reference: Available to all projects as paid company-level reference knowledge.
mount_modes.agent_specialist_reference: Visible only to selected specialist agents.
mount_modes.read_only_remote_index: Searchable through the backend without copying full content to tenant storage.
mount_modes.protected_local_index: Optional tenant-side encrypted or opaque retrieval index without readable source documents; allowed only when the license explicitly permits it.

knowledge_pack_contract.authority_policy: Paid industry knowledge can support retrieval and drafting, but it does not override tenant project documents, approved company rules, or professional human approval requirements.

usage_identity_policy.decision_policy: Every request is allowed or denied by entitlement, role, mount scope, project membership, agent permission, quota, and license status before retrieval.

entitlement_contract.failure_policy: If entitlement validation fails, keep existing approved local content available but stop new installs, updates, and remote paid retrieval.
